title: Add architecture docs for NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler design and usage
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** docs
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** Proposed for 'NetworkPolicy Sub-Reconciler
  - Ingress-Policy: n'

Create architecture documentation for the new NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler (CC-0038) covering: (1) An architecture decision record explaining the Zero-Trust/Defense-in-Depth rationale for adding NetworkPolicy to the Keystone operator — why ingress is restricted to port 5000 from user-defined selectors, why egress is auto-derived from ClusterRef fields (MariaDB:3306, Memcached:11211) plus implicit DNS, and why OpenBao egress is excluded (ESO controller handles PushSecret communication, not Keystone pods). (2) A user-facing configuration guide documenting the `spec.networkPolicy` CRD field: how to define ingress sources via `namespaceSelector`/`podSelector` pairs, how ClusterRef-based egress auto-derivation works vs. brownfield setups requiring `additionalEgress`, and example CR snippets for common scenarios (single-namespace deployment, cross-namespace ingress, brownfield database with explicit egress). (3) An operator developer reference showing the three-path nil-check lifecycle (nil+absent → NotRequired, nil+exists → delete, set → ensure), the reconcile chain position (after reconcileDeployment, before reconcileBootstrap), and the NetworkPolicyReady condition semantics. Place docs following existing patterns in architecture/docs/ for ADR and operators/keystone/ for operator-specific guides.

**Rationale:** The NetworkPolicy sub-reconciler introduces security-critical behavior that restricts network traffic to Keystone pods. Without clear documentation: (1) operators won't know they need to configure ingress sources for their deployment topology, risking lockout; (2) brownfield users won't understand they must manually add egress rules via additionalEgress since auto-derivation only works with ClusterRef; (3) the non-obvious design decision that OpenBao egress is unnecessary (because ESO handles it via PushSecrets) will be repeatedly questioned; (4) future operator developers extending the pattern to other services (Glance, Nova) won't understand the three-path lifecycle or condition contract without a reference.

**Affected Areas:**
- architecture/docs/
- operators/keystone/docs/
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** critical

---

## Open Questions

All questions resolved — answers incorporated below.

---

## Summary

Add an optional `spec.networkPolicy` field to the Keystone CRD that, when set, creates Kubernetes `NetworkPolicy` resources restricting traffic to and from Keystone API pods. Ingress is limited to port 5000 from user-defined namespace/pod selectors. Egress is auto-derived from existing spec fields (`spec.database.ClusterRef` → MariaDB:3306, `spec.cache.ClusterRef` → Memcached:11211) plus implicit DNS (UDP+TCP 53), with support for additional user-defined egress rules. This implements Zero-Trust/Defense-in-Depth for the Identity Service — the most security-sensitive OpenStack component.

---

## Resolved Design Decisions

**OpenBao egress:** Not included. Codebase confirms Keystone pods never contact OpenBao directly — `reconcile_fernet.go:320-345` and `reconcile_credential.go:312-337` create `PushSecret` CRs, and the ESO controller pod (separate namespace/deployment) handles all OpenBao communication. Users who add custom plugins that talk to OpenBao can use `additionalEgress`.

**Brownfield auto-derivation:** Not included. When `ClusterRef` is set, egress is auto-derived targeting the in-cluster Service by pod/namespace selector. When brownfield `Host`/`Port` or `Servers` is used, the target may be external or IP-based — too varied to auto-derive safely. Brownfield users must add explicit rules via `additionalEgress`.

---

## Scope

**Included:**
- `NetworkPolicySpec` struct in `api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` with `Ingress []NetworkPolicyIngressSource` (each: `NamespaceSelector map[string]string` + optional `PodSelector map[string]string`) and `AdditionalEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule` (reuse upstream type for maximum flexibility)
- Optional `NetworkPolicy *NetworkPolicySpec` pointer field on `KeystoneSpec` (nil-gated, matching `Federation *FederationSpec` pattern at line 64 and `PolicyOverrides *commonv1.PolicySpec` at line 83)
- CEL `XValidation` requiring at least one ingress source when the field is set
- `reconcileNetworkPolicy` sub-reconciler in `internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go` with condition type `NetworkPolicyReady`
- Three-path nil-check lifecycle (CC-0038 optional sub-resource pattern): nil+absent → set `NetworkPolicyReady=True` reason `NetworkPolicyNotRequired`; nil+exists → delete + set `NetworkPolicyReady=True` reason `NetworkPolicyNotRequired`; set → ensure + set `NetworkPolicyReady=True`
- Auto-derived egress from `spec.database.ClusterRef` (port 3306, resolving `<clusterRef.Name>.<namespace>.svc` via pattern from `reconcile_config.go:201-212`) and `spec.cache.ClusterRef` (port 11211, resolving `<clusterRef.Name>` via pattern from `reconcile_config.go:186-199`)
- Implicit DNS egress (UDP+TCP 53 to `kube-system`) — always included, not user-configurable
- User-configurable ingress sources (namespace/pod selector pairs) for TCP port 5000
- User-configurable `additionalEgress` for targets not auto-derived (OpenBao, brownfield DB/cache, external services)
- `"NetworkPolicyReady"` added to `subConditionTypes` in `keystone_controller.go:31-38`
- RBAC marker: `+kubebuilder:rbac:groups=networking.k8s.io,resources=networkpolicies,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete`
- `Owns(&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{})` watch in `SetupWithManager` (`keystone_controller.go:145-162`)
- Unit tests in `reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go` following existing `NewGomegaWithT(t)` / `fake.NewClientBuilder()` patterns (with `networkingv1.AddToScheme`)

**Excluded:**
- OpenBao egress by default (ESO controller handles OpenBao communication via PushSecret CRs, not Keystone pods; can be added via `additionalEgress`)
- NetworkPolicy for CronJob pods (fernet/credential rotation — different pod selector `app.kubernetes.io/component: fernet-rotation` etc., separate concern, not requested)
- NetworkPolicy for db_sync/bootstrap Job pods (short-lived, separate concern)
- Egress to Kubernetes API server (Keystone API pods don't call the K8s API; rotation CronJobs do but are out of scope)
- Brownfield auto-derivation (external host:port targets are too varied; users configure via `additionalEgress`)
- Common helper in `internal/common/deployment/` (NetworkPolicy construction is Keystone-specific due to auto-derived rules; generalize when a second operator needs it)
- Webhook validation (kubebuilder markers + CEL suffice, consistent with existing approach)
- `spec.networkPolicy.enabled` boolean toggle (pointer-nil semantics already provide enable/disable)

---

## Visualization

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    R["Reconcile Loop"] --> S["reconcileSecrets"]
    S --> C["reconcileConfig"]
    C --> F["reconcileFernetKeys"]
    F --> CK["reconcileCredentialKeys"]
    CK --> DB["reconcileDatabase"]
    DB --> D["reconcileDeployment"]
    D --> NP["reconcileNetworkPolicy #40;NEW#41;"]
    NP --> B["reconcileBootstrap"]
    B --> RDY["setReadyCondition"]

    style NP fill:#2d6a4f,stroke:#1b4332,color:#fff
```

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph Ingress["Ingress Rules"]
        SRC["Allowed Namespaces/Pods"] -->|"TCP 5000"| KS["Keystone API Pods"]
    end

    subgraph Egress["Egress Rules #40;auto-derived from ClusterRef#41;"]
        KS -->|"TCP 3306"| MDB["MariaDB Service"]
        KS -->|"TCP 11211"| MC["Memcached Service"]
        KS -->|"UDP+TCP 53"| DNS["kube-dns"]
    end

    subgraph Extra["Additional Egress #40;user-defined#41;"]
        KS -.->|"configurable"| OB["OpenBao / brownfield DB / other"]
    end
```

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> NoPolicy: spec.networkPolicy is nil
    NoPolicy --> PolicyCreated: spec.networkPolicy set
    PolicyCreated --> PolicyReady: reconciled successfully
    PolicyReady --> PolicyUpdated: spec.networkPolicy changed
    PolicyUpdated --> PolicyReady: reconciled
    PolicyReady --> NoPolicy: spec.networkPolicy removed
    PolicyCreated --> NoPolicy: spec.networkPolicy removed
```

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph OpenBao Communication
        CJ["Rotation CronJob Pod"] -->|"PATCH Secret via K8s API"| KSec["Kubernetes Secret"]
        ESO["ESO Controller Pod #40;separate namespace#41;"] -->|"watches"| PS["PushSecret CR"]
        ESO -->|"pushes keys to"| OB["OpenBao"]
        PS -.->|"references"| KSec
    end

    subgraph No OpenBao Egress Needed
        KPod["Keystone API Pod"] -->|"reads mounted"| KSec
        CJ -->|"reads mounted"| KSec
    end

    style ESO fill:#d4a574,stroke:#8b6914,color:#000
    style KPod fill:#4a90d9,stroke:#2d5f8a,color:#fff
```

---

## Key Components

- **`NetworkPolicySpec`** (`api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go`): New struct with `Ingress []NetworkPolicyIngressSource` (each containing `NamespaceSelector map[string]string` + optional `PodSelector map[string]string`) and `AdditionalEgress []networkingv1.NetworkPolicyEgressRule` (reuse upstream type for maximum flexibility). Added as optional `NetworkPolicy *NetworkPolicySpec` pointer on `KeystoneSpec` (matching `Federation *FederationSpec` at line 64). CEL `XValidation` requiring at least one ingress source.
- **`reconcileNetworkPolicy`** (`internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go`): New sub-reconciler following the CC-0038 optional sub-resource lifecycle. If `spec.networkPolicy` nil → delete existing + set `NetworkPolicyReady=True` reason `NetworkPolicyNotRequired`. If set → build NetworkPolicy with auto-derived egress + user ingress/additional egress → `CreateOrUpdate` with `SetControllerReference` → set `NetworkPolicyReady=True`.
- **`buildNetworkPolicy`** (same file): Constructs `networkingv1.NetworkPolicy` targeting pods via `selectorLabels()` (`app.kubernetes.io/name=keystone`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance={name}`, from `reconcile_deployment.go:120-126`). Ingress: port 5000 TCP from user-defined sources. Egress: auto-derived from `spec.database.ClusterRef` (port 3306, resolved as `<name>.<namespace>.svc`) and `spec.cache.ClusterRef` (port 11211, resolved as `<name>`) + DNS (UDP+TCP 53) + user additional rules. Uses `commonLabels()` on ObjectMeta.
- **Controller integration** (`keystone_controller.go`): Add `"NetworkPolicyReady"` to `subConditionTypes` (lines 31-38). Add RBAC marker for `networking.k8s.io/networkpolicies`. Add `Owns(&networkingv1.NetworkPolicy{})` to `SetupWithManager` (lines 145-162). Call `reconcileNetworkPolicy` after `reconcileDeployment` in the reconcile chain.
- **Tests** (`reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go`): Unit tests using `NewGomegaWithT(t)` + `fake.NewClientBuilder().WithScheme(s).WithObjects(ks).Build()` pattern (with `networkingv1.AddToScheme`). Coverage: policy creation with correct pod selector/ingress/egress rules, auto-derivation of MariaDB+Memcached egress from ClusterRef, DNS egress always present, additional user-defined egress included, no auto-derived egress when brownfield-only (requires `additionalEgress`), policy deletion when `spec.networkPolicy` removed, skip with `NetworkPolicyNotRequired` when nil, owner reference set correctly.
